pi_items.source: Task specification - reasonable naming convention for Pacific Northwest VFR tour plan

FAA AeroNav documentation and flight training sources confirm VFR Sectional charts are the standard for VFR training flights and cross-country navigation. Avare documentation confirms support for Sectional charts.
AirNav and official sources confirm KSEA is Seattle-Tacoma International Airport, a major commercial airport serving Seattle metropolitan area, located in Washington state. Class B airspace.
AirNav and official sources confirm KPDX is Portland International Airport, the largest airport in Oregon located in Portland, OR. Class C airspace.
AirNav and airport authority sources confirm KEUG is Mahlon Sweet Field / Eugene Airport, located 7 miles northwest of Eugene, Oregon. Has operating FAA control tower.
AirNav and official sources confirm KMFR is Rogue Valley International–Medford Airport, located 3 miles north of downtown Medford in Jackson County, Oregon. Class D airspace.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real Pacific Northwest airports forming a classic VFR training route: KSEA (Seattle-Tacoma, Class B) in the north progressing south through KPDX (Portland, Class C), KEUG (Eugene, control tower), and KMFR (Medford, Class D). VFR Sectional charts are the FAA-standard chart type for visual flight training and cross-country navigation. All four airports are real, public-use facilities with verified ICAO codes and actual airspace designations.
